growth_records.lvlwhy: species-level trait compilation (Madin) — no strain recorded in source
growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: corkrey.
growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h computed as ln(2)/doubling_time_h. Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.lvlwhy: the source paper (often a review/modeling reuse) states no strain
growth_records.cite: Rabbers I et al. Metabolism at Evolutionary Optimal States. Metabolites (2015). DOI:10.3390/metabo5020311. [PMC4495375]
growth_records.snippet: Thermobrachium celere (10 min [95]). As a general tendency, fast generation times seem to be reported mainly in thermophilic organisms.
growth_records.notes: Review-cited generation time (primary source = ref [95]). Reported generation time 10 min = 0.16667 h; calculated mu = ln2/0.16667 = 4.16 h-1. No medium/temperature/pH given.
